source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Uncontrolled gas release and eye injury
event.incident_type: Loss of Containment with Personnel Injury
actions_taken: Injured party proceeded to eye wash station immediately
Person in Charge activated Emergency Shut Down (ESD)
Medic continued face and eye washing for approximately 40 minutes
Field Helicopter transported injured party to hospital
Corneal Surgeon removed debris from left eye on 25-Nov-2014
Second surgery performed on 26-Nov-2014 to remove additional debris
event.summary: On 24-Nov-2014 at approximately 0830, an operator at Fieldwood Energy's Matagorda Island 622 'C' platform was injured when investigating a flow rate indication problem on the #4 Production Separator. While attempting to verify orifice plate installation, the operator failed to close the equalizer valve between the upper and lower chambers of the Senior Orifice Fitting prior to loosening clamp bar bolts. A plugged blow-down valve created a false impression of zero pressure. When the operator loosened the second clamp bar bolt, approximately 915 PSIG of natural gas and debris was released, striking the operator's left eye and face. The operator was transported by helicopter to hospital where corneal surgeries were performed. Full recovery is expected.
key_phrases: blow down valve plugged with debris and/or frozen hydrates
procedure not reevaluated for abnormal condition

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in Senior Orifice Fitting

threats.name: Failure to close equalizer valve before disassembly
Inadequate pressure verification before equipment opening

consequences.name: Personnel eye and facial injury from gas and debris
Loss of time injury requiring hospitalization and surgery

controls.name: Orifice Plate Removal Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure safe disassembly of pressurized equipment
controls.lod_basis: Written procedure should prevent hazardous conditions before they develop

supporting_text: The IP failed to close the equalizer valve between the upper (top) and lower (bottom) chamber on the SOF prior to loosening the clamp bar bolts on the upper chamber
When the IP found the SOF in an abnormal condition, all steps in OP removal procedure should have been reevaluated

controls.name: Blow-down Valve for Pressure Relief
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to verify zero pressure before disassembly
controls.lod_basis: Designed to relieve pressure and confirm safe conditions

supporting_text: The blow down valve on the upper chamber of the SOF plugged with debris and/or frozen hydrates when it was opened to atmosphere. This resulted in the false impression that the upper chamber of the SOF had bled to zero pressure.
The blow down valve on the upper chamber of the SOF relieved briefly and the IP assumed there was no pressure remaining

controls.name: Safety Glasses / Personal Protective Equipment
controls.barrier_role: PPE to reduce severity of eye and facial injury
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: The IP was wearing safety glasses at the time of the incident.
Natural gas and debris blew upward striking the IP on the left side of his face and left eye

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) System
controls.barrier_role: Automated system to stop operations and mitigate consequences
controls.lod_basis: Designed to halt operations when emergency conditions detected

supporting_text: The PIC heard the gas release and immediately reported to the scene. The PIC activated the Emergency Shut Down (ESD)

controls.name: On-site Medical Response and Eye Wash Station
controls.barrier_role: Immediate medical intervention to reduce injury severity
controls.lod_basis: Recovery measure to minimize injury consequences

supporting_text: The IP immediately went to the eye wash station and began to irrigate his face and left eye.
The Medic continued face and eye washing procedures for approximately 40 minutes until the Field Helicopter arrived

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
